Diary
  1. Home
  2. Activities
  3. Diary
  4. The American Dream | Saturday Classics
  • 06 Jun 26
    upcoming

    The American Dream | Saturday Classics

    Saturday, 6 June 2026 - 18:00

    Advance ticket sales: 18.00€ / Price at the entrance: 18.00€

    Tickets Personalize your visit
    • Description

    Soprano Indira Ferrer and pianist Carles Puig bring this magnificent repertoire to life in a concert built around 20th-century American music, featuring works by some of the most emblematic composers of the American continent such as Dominick Argento, Aaron Copland, Duke Ellington, and George Gershwin, to name just a few.

    We will also revisit several Catalan composers, presenting somewhat lesser-known melodies of very singular beauty, who were able to succeed in distant lands while pursuing the beautiful American dream, having to move away from their beloved homeland for various personal reasons. There they achieved fame and success, becoming celebrated composers, and today we will give them their place in this recital, “The American Dream.”

    On this occasion, we will feature the artists:

    • Indira Ferrer, soprano.
    • Carles Puig, piano.

    WORKS OF:  Copland, Gershwin, Ellington, Casals, ...

    PROGRAM:

    CATALAN COMPOSERS WHO COULD HAVE SUCCEEDED IN AMERICA

    • Cançó de mar ............................................................... Lluïsa Casagemas

    • En el mirall canviant de la mar blava .............................. Pau Casals

    • Come to the Land of Peace ........................................... Jaume Nunó

    AMERICAN COMPOSERS

    • Canción .......................................................................... Dominick Argento

    • Simple Gifts ..................................................................... Aaron Copland

    • I Bought Me a Cat ........................................................ Aaron Copland

    Instrumental piece

    • In My Solitude ............................................................. George Gershwin

    • Mood Indigo ................................................................ Duke Ellington

    • Smoke Gets in Your Eyes (from the musical Show Boat) .... Jerome Kern

    • On My Own (from Les Misérables) .............................. Claude-Michel Schönberg

    • Embraceable You ........................................................ George Gershwin

    • I’ve Got Rhythm .......................................................... George Gershwin

    FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS):

    • All tickets include access to both the exhibitions and the concert.

    • Seats are assigned according to the order of reservation. No seat changes will be allowed once assigned.

    • Entry to the concert hall will take place between 17:50 and 18:00. Once the concert begins, no further entry will be allowed.

    • The exhibition can be visited either before or after the concert, keeping in mind that the museum closes at 20:00.

    • The exhibition tour takes approximately 45 minutes.

    • Showing your ticket or reservation on your mobile device is sufficient; printing is not necessary.

    • The museum reserves the right to cancel any unpaid reservation 5 minutes before the start of the concert. Therefore, we recommend arriving at the box office 30 minutes early.

    • For advance ticket purchases: no refunds will be available after 18:00. If the user has not notified the museum of their delay or absence before the concert, the ticket may be reassigned to another visitor starting from 18:00.

This event is framed within

Saturday Classics

Concerts

MEAM is not just a museum, we’re the spot where contemporary figurative art comes alive and culture feels fresher than ever. Our institutional commitment goes far beyond hanging paintings;...
